function.name: computer
function.description: Control a computer GUI using screenshot-local pixel coordinates. Coordinates refer to the latest screenshot image provided by the client, with (0, 0) at the top-left corner.

action.description: The computer action to perform. Action-specific requirements: screenshot and cursor_position require no extra fields; wait may include duration_ms; move requires x and y; click and double_click require x and y and may include button and modifiers; drag requires path and may include modifiers; scroll requires x, y, scroll_x, and scroll_y and may include modifiers; keypress requires keys; type requires text.

target.description: Concise, distinct short label describing the visible UI target or immediate purpose of this action for logging, such as "Pause button", "Name field", "page to load", or "refresh screen".

x.description: X coordinate in pixels relative to the top-left corner of the latest screenshot image.

y.description: Y coordinate in pixels relative to the top-left corner of the latest screenshot image.

button.description: Mouse button to click. Used with click and double_click. Defaults to left when omitted.

modifiers.description: Keyboard modifiers to hold during mouse actions on macOS. Use option for Option and command for Command.

path.description: Drag path as screenshot-local pixel coordinates. Used with drag.

x.description: X coordinate in pixels relative to the top-left corner of the latest screenshot image.

y.description: Y coordinate in pixels relative to the top-left corner of the latest screenshot image.

scroll_x.description: Horizontal scroll amount. Negative scrolls left, positive scrolls right.

scroll_y.description: Vertical scroll amount. Negative scrolls up, positive scrolls down.

keys.description: Keys to press together for keypress, such as ["CTRL", "L"] or ["ENTER"].

text.description: Text to type at the current keyboard focus. Used with type.

duration_ms.description: Time to wait in milliseconds. Used with wait. The executor should choose a small default when omitted.
